Harden client reconnect receive lifecycle against repeated reset loops
Close-frame reconnect boundary (Task 2 follow-up after first failed UAT)
Emit close frame on every ws teardown branch (fix raw-reset class of failure)
Add 5s server ws keepalive ping against proxy idle-close (fix clean-close-after-idle class of failure)
Validate live browser disconnect and race behavior (human-verify checkpoint)
completed_tasks.progress: Iteration 2 code + automated verify done; awaiting live browser UAT approval from user

remaining_tasks.name: Re-run live browser UAT against 434e2ca and confirm all checkpoint criteria (or produce fresh failing logs)
If approved: create 26.1-03-SUMMARY.md, run code-review gate, regression gate, schema-drift gate, verify-phase, update ROADMAP/STATE/PROJECT.md, close parent UAT gaps (phase 26)

blockers.description: Task 3 human-verify checkpoint (blocking gate) cannot self-approve per .continue-here.md blocking anti-pattern: completion must be gated on live UAT, not static evidence.
blockers.workaround: User must run dx serve --package iron_hermes_ui and manually verify against 6 criteria in the latest checkpoint payload.

blockers.description: Two successive live-UAT failures have revealed two distinct root causes (raw TCP reset → close-frame fix; idle-close after 9s → keepalive fix). A third failure mode is possible if neither fix is sufficient in combination.
blockers.workaround: If next UAT fails, user must paste any [server] warn lines (especially 'websocket keepalive ping failed; closing connection') so the next iteration has transport-failure evidence rather than speculation.

human_actions_pending.action: Run dx serve --package iron_hermes_ui and execute Task 3 manual verification steps against commit 434e2ca
human_actions_pending.context: This is the blocking human-verify checkpoint for plan 26.1-03. Phase 26.1 cannot be marked complete without either 'approved' or fresh failing logs from this step.

decisions.decision: Use server-side Message::Ping every 5s (not client-side, not custom reconnect backoff) for keepalive
decisions.rationale: Browsers auto-respond with Pong at WS protocol level, so no warp_hermes.rs change is required and D-10 (automatic-reconnect-only) + D-11 (scoped transient-state reset) stay intact. 5s is well under common proxy idle thresholds (typical ~10s).

decisions.decision: Classify failed keepalive Ping as a send-path failure per D-05
decisions.rationale: A Ping that cannot be delivered proves the transport is broken, which matches D-05's broken-send semantics: abort in-flight turn, emit CloseCode::Away with reason 'keepalive failed', break loop.

decisions.decision: Use tokio::time::interval with MissedTickBehavior::Skip and consume the first immediate tick before entering the select! loop
decisions.rationale: Prevents a Ping burst at connect and prevents back-to-back catch-up Pings after long select! wake gaps.
A half-broken transport may already reject the close frame; teardown must not block on its delivery (respects D-06's intent not to hang on broken-send paths).

decisions.decision: D-01..D-15 lifecycle decisions treated as immutable throughout plan 26.1-03
decisions.rationale: Locked by user in 26.1-CONTEXT.md. Never redesign protocol, route path, reconnect strategy, or in-flight-gate location. All fixes must fit inside that contract.

next_action: User runs dx serve --package iron_hermes_ui and manually executes the 6 verification steps in the latest checkpoint payload (idle check, stream check, race check, regression check on prior signatures). On approval: run /gsd-resume-work, then continue /gsd-execute-phase 26.1 --gaps-only to drive through code-review gate → regression gate → schema-drift gate → verify-phase → update-roadmap → close parent UAT gaps for phase 26.
context_notes: Plan 26.1-03 has gone through 4 distinct fix iterations against live UAT. Each iteration peeled one layer of the onion. Iteration 1-2 (Tasks 1-2) addressed server teardown classification and client disconnect dedupe. Iteration 3 (close-frame fix e950d71) eliminated the raw TCP reset signature. Iteration 4 (keepalive 434e2ca) addresses the ~9s idle-close signature exposed after iteration 3. The next UAT run tests whether (a) both fixes together are sufficient, or (b) there is a third root cause. The ws.rs file is now 350+ lines of carefully-balanced lifecycle code — any future changes should read the current state end-to-end and reason against D-01..D-15 before editing. Staging discipline is critical: the worktree has 300+ unrelated dirty entries.
